name: Skybox
description: A utility behavior that configures mesh objects as skyboxes by disabling physics, shadow casting/receiving, and making materials transparent. Essential for creating background environments like skies, horizons, or space scenes that should render behind all geometry without interfering with game physics or lighting.
author: stemstudio
documentation:
## Skybox

A utility behavior that configures mesh objects as skyboxes by disabling physics, shadow casting/receiving, and making materials transparent. Essential for creating background environments like skies, horizons, or space scenes that should render behind all geometry without interfering with game physics or lighting.

### Key Features
- Automatic physics disabling on skybox object
- Shadow casting/receiving removal for all children
- Material transparency enabling for proper rendering
- Recursive traversal of all child meshes
- Editor and runtime mode support
- Works with array materials and single materials
- Physics body removal from physics engine
- Preserves original mesh structure

### How It Works
1. Attach to skybox mesh object (typically large sphere or cube)
2. On add (game or editor mode):
3. Disables physics: sets target.userData.physics.enabled = false
4. Calls removePhysicsObjectBody() to remove from physics engine
5. Traverses target and all children recursively
6. For each object in hierarchy:
   - Sets castShadow = false (no shadow casting)
   - Sets receiveShadow = false (no shadow receiving)
7. For each Mesh child:
   - Checks if material exists
   - Handles both single material and material arrays
   - Sets material.transparent = true for each material
8. Skybox now renders as background without physics or lighting interference
9. Typically used with RenderOrder or background scene settings

### Common Use Cases
- Sky domes or sky spheres for outdoor scenes
- Space backgrounds with stars and nebulae
- Horizon meshes in open-world games
- Atmospheric effects layers
- Backdrop environments for level boundaries
- Cubemap skyboxes (6-sided cube)
- Distant mountain or city skylines
- Underwater caustics or fog layers
- Procedurally generated sky systems
- Day/night cycle background layers

### When Not To Use
- For scene.background property (use THREE.CubeTexture instead)
- When skybox needs physics interaction
- For objects that should cast or receive shadows
- When transparency isn't desired
- For 2D sprite-based skyboxes (use different approach)
- When using advanced sky shaders (DayNightCycle behavior better)
- For volumetric atmospheric effects (use fog or post-processing)
- When skybox should occlude objects (defeats purpose)
- For UI elements or HUD overlays
- When multiple skyboxes need different transparency settings

### Technical Details
- Physics removal: game.app.physics.removePhysicsObjectBody(target)
- userData.physics.enabled set to false to prevent re-addition
- Traversal: target.traverse(child => ...) for recursive processing
- Shadow properties: castShadow and receiveShadow set to false
- Material transparency: material.transparent = true on all materials
- Array material handling: Array.isArray(child.material) check
- Type checking: child instanceof Mesh for material access
- No throttle configuration (init-only behavior)
- Runs in both onAdded() and onEditorAdded() for consistency
- No update loop required (one-time configuration)
- Assumes target is Mesh or contains Mesh children
- Material changes persist until behavior removed or manually reverted
- Does not affect geometry, only material and physics properties
